mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) when either series trends downward.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production outpaces capacity constraints during late-cycle expansions, generating supply bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle periods with rising INDPRO, or the mechanism shows no predictive alpha (Rank IC < 0.03) in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of central bank policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample directional tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates underlying demand resilience that markets initially underprice, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a twenty-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.03) when GDP growth falls below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving labor market fundamentals and rising consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of recovery-phase months with falling UNRATE, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) in regime-specific walk-forward tests.
